assumptions: The intragroup dividend receivable is a recognised monetary item denominated in a foreign currency.
The identified foreign exchange exposure gives rise to exchange differences that are not fully eliminated on consolidation and can affect consolidated profit or loss.

approaches.label: Fair value hedge of the foreign currency risk on the intragroup receivable
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: This is a genuine hedge designation route because IFRS 9 permits a recognised asset to be hedged for a particular risk, and specifically allows the foreign currency risk of an intragroup monetary item in consolidated financial statements when the resulting exchange differences are not fully eliminated.

approaches.label: Cash flow hedge of the foreign currency settlement cash flows
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: This is a distinct hedge route because IFRS 9 defines a cash flow hedge as a hedge of variability in cash flows of a recognised asset or liability, which can capture the settlement cash flows of the foreign-currency intragroup receivable.
